section: Class 10 Economics - Chapter 4: Globalisation and the Indian Economy

notes.summary: Globalisation means increasing connection between countries through trade, investment and technology. It allows goods and services to move easily across borders.
notes.importance: Helps students understand how countries are economically connected today.

notes.summary: MNCs are companies that operate in more than one country. They set up factories and offices in different nations to increase profits.
notes.importance: Explains how big companies influence global trade.

notes.summary: Foreign trade is the exchange of goods and services between countries. It includes exports (selling goods abroad) and imports (buying goods from other countries).
notes.importance: Shows how countries depend on each other for goods.

notes.summary: In 1991, India removed trade restrictions and opened its markets to foreign companies. This process is called liberalisation.
notes.importance: Marks a major change in India’s economic policy.

notes.summary: WTO is an international organisation that sets rules for global trade and ensures smooth trade between countries.
Globalisation provides consumers with more choices and better quality goods. However, small producers may face competition from large MNCs.
notes.importance: Highlights both positive and negative effects of globalisation.
